title: 19th ICE Detainee Death in 2026: Second Death at Winn in Under Two Months
excerpt: Mamuka Artmeladze, a 43-year-old Georgian national with no criminal record, was found unresponsive and died in ICE custody at Winn Correctional Center in Louisiana — making him the 19th ICE detainee death in 2026 and the second at Winn in less than two months. The death came just two days after the DHS OIG published a report documenting banned chokeholds and medical failures at the same facility.
content:
On June 4, 2026, Mamuka Artmeladze, a 43-year-old man from the country of Georgia, was found unresponsive at Winn Correctional Center in Winnfield, Louisiana. Staff initiated lifesaving measures, but he was pronounced dead less than an hour after being transported by ambulance to Winn Parish Medical Center. His cause of death remained pending an autopsy.

  • Artmeladze's death was the 19th recorded in ICE custody since January 1, 2026 — a pace on track to surpass the all-time record of 32 set in 2004.
  • He had no criminal record and had been detained at Winn for nearly four months after ICE determined he no longer had lawful status.
  • His death occurred just two days after the OIG published a report documenting banned chokeholds, a pen-stabbing incident, and medical record failures at the same facility.
  • Winn is operated by LaSalle Corrections under contract with ICE and holds more than 1,500 male detainees at any given time.

The second death at Winn in under two months intensified calls from advocates and members of Congress for independent oversight of ICE detention conditions and immediate improvements in medical care.
